title: [InstCombine] Missed optimization for add + and + icmp pattern
summary:
The original motivating case is: with a `range`-constrained input, a small set of switch values can be recognized as “all values where certain bits are set,” even if that is not obvious from contiguous ranges.  
For `range(i8 0, 4)`, the accepted values are `{1, 3}`; that is exactly the values in-range with bit `0` set. InstCombine/InstructionSimplify can express this as `(x & 1) == 1`, and the issue was that the switch/select lowering only handled older formulations (for example, min-relative bitmask patterns), missing this bit-conjunction form.

The patch generalizes this by adding a second recognition strategy in `foldSwitchToSelect()` for power-of-two-sized case sets. It computes:
- `AndMask = AND` of all case constants,
- `KnownBits` of the condition,
- the number of “free” varying bits allowed by the known range.
If:
- `AndMask != 0`,
- the mask is feasible under known bits,
- and the number of free bits equals `log2(case_count)`,
then the cases are exactly those values where all bits in `AndMask` are set, so it lowers to:
`select ((Cond & AndMask) == AndMask), TrueVal, DefaultVal`.

The dev tests broaden coverage from the single `{1,3}` example to:
- different ranges (`0..4`, `1..4`, `0..8`, `0..16`);
- different masks/bit positions (odd bit, top bit, middle two bits, `6/7/14/15`);
- negative cases where case count is wrong, mask shape is wrong, values are out of range, no `range` metadata exists, or corner/singleton cases should not match.  
This is a good template: derive a canonical bit-property from case constants, then use known-bits/range info to prove exactness.
